title: Immunolabeling of a Whole Mouse Brain
author: Rajvi Javeri

steps.instruction: Transfer the brain to a small 4 mL Shorty Vial containing diluted primary antibody in ~ 4 mL PTxw and incubate with rocking, a nutator, or shaker at 4 °C for 11 days. 15 μg/brain for primary antibody 30 μg/brain for primary conjugated antibody

Transfer the brain to a 20 mL Liquid Scintillation vial and wash with PTxw, rocking on a nutating mixer or shaker at Room temperature , and replacing solution for each step: PTxw for 01:00:00 PTxw for 02:00:00 PTxw for 04:00:00 PTxw Overnight PTxw for 24:00:00 PTxw for 24:00:00 PTxw for 24:00:00, +
Using a 20 mL glass vial, wash the brain with PTxw, rotating at Room temperature Overnight .

Spin down the desired secondary antibody in a centrifuge set to 10.000 rpm, °C, for 00:01:00 and use the supernatant for immunolabeling. Transfer the brain to a small 4 mL Shorty vial containing diluted secondary antibody in ~ 4 mL PTxw and incubate with rocking at 4 °C for 11 days. 30 μg/brain for secondary antibody

Prepare a new solution of PTxw containing 200mM NaCl and 0.1% Tween, and only use this new solution for the remaining washes. Transfer brain to a 20 mL Liquid Scintillation vial and wash with new PTxw, rocking at Room temperature and replacing solution for each step: PTxw for 01:00:00 PTxw for 02:00:00 PTxw for 04:00:00 PTxw Overnight PTxw for 24:00:00 PTxw for 24:00:00 PTxw for 24:00:00, +
Wash the brain with 1X PBS, rocking at Room temperature Overnight .
Store the immunolabeled brain in 0.01% PBS Azide at 4 °C
Start with a whole mouse brain perfused with 4% PFA, post-fixed, and stored in PBS in a 20 mL vial.
